methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $95M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Mariana Minerals is a Series A climate and energy company founded by Turner Caldwell that has raised $95M+ to date. The valuation assessment draws on a comp universe of 9 same-sector peers: 1 public company (Linz, LNZA) and 8 private firms including Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen.

A peer-median approach was not applicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the Climate & Energy sector—while 9 total peers exist, fewer than 3 met narrow filtering criteria around stage and valuation tier, making sector-wide medians unreliable. Accordingly, analysis relied on stage-progression methodology, applying a standard 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement to the $95M total raised proxy, yielding an implied valuation of $190M.

The triangulated range spans $162M (low) to $218M (high), centered on $190M. Given the single-method foundation, this range carries material uncertainty around both the appropriate multiplier for Mariana's specific risk profile and the relevance of the available peer set to this company's technology and market dynamics. Key sensitivities include the stage advancement multiplier assumption and the absence of tighter, same-stage comparable data. Additional founder background and operational metrics would strengthen conviction in final valuation placement within this band.
